body.title: uushowticket + uuticketadd: terminal ticket inspection and annotation
body.result: uushowticket.py (human-readable ticket display) + uuticketadd.py (timestamped note append). ~/bin/ wrappers for both.
body.intention: I intend that Akien can read and annotate any ticket from the terminal without a CC session — uushowticket pretty-prints the full ticket, uuticketadd appends a timestamped note.
body.description:
Two complementary commands for terminal-based ticket triage without chat overhead.

`uushowticket <id>` — pretty-prints a ticket for human reading:
  Title, status, size, tags, priority, worker, intention, description (full, not JSON).
  Cleaner than cc_queue.py show which dumps raw JSON.

`uuticketadd <id> <note>` — appends a timestamped note to the ticket's description:
  Appends `\n\n**Note [YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM]:** <note>` to the description in Postgres.
  Returns confirmation. Use for: recording a failed approach, a discovered dependency, a scope change.

Bash functions in ~/.bashrc pointing to lab/claudecode/uushowticket.py and lab/claudecode/uuticketadd.py.

**Affected files:** lab/claudecode/uushowticket.py, lab/claudecode/uuticketadd.py, ~/.bashrc
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; Postgres via cc_queue.py or direct psql
**Scope boundary:** IN: read + append-note. OUT: status changes (cc_queue.py setstatus), full edit (that's a sprint).
**Completion criteria:** `uushowticket T-uurecall-fulltext` prints human-readable ticket without JSON noise. `uuticketadd T-uurecall-fulltext 'tested, also misses palace nodes'` appends note and confirms. Both exit 1 with message on unknown ticket ID.

**Note [2026-06-08 11:00]:** smoke test note — testing uuticketadd works
